name: PER_INTG_INBOUND_DETAILS
description: This table stores Details about the each record that was received.

columns.Name: INBOUND_DETAIL_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifer to identify the row.

columns.Name: INBOUND_OBJECT_NAME
columns.Comments: Data object that wasn transferred and resulted in success in Partner system

columns.Name: INBOUND_OBJECT_KEY
columns.Comments: Primay Key of the object. For example ,Candidate identifier,Requisition Identifier

columns.Name: ESS_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: ESS job request Identifier that user submitted

columns.Name: INBOUND_DATA_ID
columns.Comments: Inbound data identifier from the parent table PER_INTG_INBOUND_DATA

columns.Name: MESSAGE_TYPE
columns.Comments: Type of the message recieved from Partner

columns.Name: MESSAGE_DESCRIPTION
columns.Comments: message Description that partner sends back to Fusion

columns.Name: BUSINESS_GROUP_ID
columns.Comments: enterprise identifier for the mulit tenant purpose

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
